title: CacheStore.warm_up(db, content_ids, pool) for parallel cache hydration
description: Add a warm-up method that walks a content_id list, identifies rows missing or stale in CacheStore, and populates them in parallel via the shared pool. Used by the startup pre-warm pipeline (TASK-027).
acceptanceCriteria: CacheStore.warm_up(db, content_ids: list[int], pool: ThreadPoolExecutor, progress_cb: Callable[[int,int],None] | None) populates missing energy_curve, classification, similarity_vector, mixability rows.
warm_up is interruptible via a threading.Event passed in cancel_event kwarg.
Progress callback fires after every K (=50) tracks with (done, total).
warm_up does not re-attempt rows with anlz_mtime = -1 sentinel.
warm_up does not block readers: WAL mode + per-row commits keep readers unblocked throughout.

steps.details: Pre-compute which content_ids need work by querying CacheStore for the union of (energy_curve, classification, similarity_vector, mixability) rows matching expected_anlz_mtime. Anything not in the union is a 'needs work' candidate. Skip rows with anlz_mtime = -1.

Define _warm_one(content_id, db) -> tuple of (curve_or_None, classification_or_None, similarity_or_None, mixability_or_None, anlz_mtime). Computes each missing entry via the existing analysis modules (with set_cache_store already wired so the result also populates L1 + L2). Writer thread drains results and commits CacheStore rows in batches of 100 inside a single transaction (BEGIN; INSERT OR REPLACE x100; COMMIT).

Wrap the as_completed loop with a counter; call progress_cb(done, total) every 50 completions. Check cancel_event at every iteration; on set, drain in-flight futures (don't cancel them - let them write what they have) and exit.
tests/test_cache.py: (a) warm_up on a 100-track mocked db populates all 4 tables; (b) cancel_event mid-run leaves a partial cache (no exception); (c) warm_up is idempotent - second call is a no-op.

technicalNotes: Batched per-100 commits are a perf compromise - per-row commits would create excessive WAL churn during warm-up.
Avoid calling warm_up on the request thread - it runs only from serve/deps.py:_prewarm_index (TASK-027) on a daemon thread.
